text: In response to all-out maximal exertion lasting 30-120 seconds, peak blood lactate values of approximately 15-25 mM may be observed 3-8 minutes post-exercise.
The lactate threshold is a better predictor of endurance performance than VO2max.
The lactate threshold is a better indicator of exercise intensity than heart rate.
The lactate threshold is useful for prescribing exercise intensities for both diseased and non-diseased populations.
During progressive incremental exercise, blood lactate increases gradually at first and then exponentially as exercise intensity increases beyond the lactate threshold.

At rest and during most exercise conditions, whole blood lactate values are on average 70% of corresponding plasma lactate values, requiring careful interpretation of which sample type is being measured.
